subject: Update css file name in conf.py
commit_message:
Update css file name in conf.py

ribbon.css is the correct name of the css file.

Issue-ID: CIMAN-376
Signed-off-by: Jessica Wagantall <jwagantall@linuxfoundation.org>
Change-Id: I178885a24a32ee1aa45508a38275e48ff6c71a1d

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

Gitlab controller: Deployment put on queue, number 4 on the queue.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-2

And here we go... BUM!

Less than two months since we merge:

https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/101311

I've been complaining about this solution multiple times but no one from LFN was willing to take those comments into account "because we do it this way in all other projects" and "those are static file that will never be updated". As @Sylvain wrote in the review:
"I also think this is a bad solution and I keep my "I todd you so" for when I'll blow. __BUT__ everybody has moved except us so I'll merge. See you in six months / one year when this solution will fail"

So here we are after not 6 but only 2 months with those "static files" and having ~100 reviews:

https://gerrit.onap.org/r/q/CIMAN-376

Just to change which XSS file should be used. Don't you think sth is really wrong here? and if we merge this what we do with Frankfurt? Cherry-pick and have another 100 reviews?

*I'm really looking forward to have some proper solution for this as it has just been proven that the one which has been force pushed doesn't work*

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-2
> 
> And here we go... BUM!
> 
> Less than two months since we merge:
> 
> https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/101311
> 
> I've been complaining about this solution multiple times but no one from LFN was willing to take those comments into account "because we do it this way in all other projects" and "those are static file that will never be updated". As @Sylvain wrote in the review:
> "I also think this is a bad solution and I keep my "I todd you so" for when I'll blow. __BUT__ everybody has moved except us so I'll merge. See you in six months / one year when this solution will fail"
> 
> So here we are after not 6 but only 2 months with those "static files" and having ~100 reviews:
> 
> https://gerrit.onap.org/r/q/CIMAN-376
> 
> Just to change which XSS file should be used. Don't you think sth is really wrong here? and if we merge this what we do with Frankfurt? Cherry-pick and have another 100 reviews?
> 
> *I'm really looking forward to have some proper solution for this as it has just been proven that the one which has been force pushed doesn't work*

Hi Krzy, 

We all understood your frustration. I agree this migration has this part as a downside but the benefits are so much better compared to submodules. 
Changes to config files are not done too often and I am willing to take the workload whenever it happens, I have committed to it. These changes were done as part of a script and did not take too much of my time to make.
It will take me some time to +2 and merge all, correct. But is something that I can help doing meanwhile we continue to make things better by adapting things to my script. We can think of adding a direct push rather than gerrit changes if think changes are small enough for example (not an official solution, im just providing an example. I would appreciate if you and the community provide their examples too on how can all make things better for docs and for teams). 
For now, I don't see why this should be -2. Can you please provide a justification on why is this not a good change right now?

As agreed in the TSC, this is not needed for Frankfurt and teams do not need to cherry-pick this change. This change will be good to have in G release however hence the reason why I am merging it now so that teams will inherit it when branching for G

Patch Set 1:

Gitlab Azure 2: Chained-CI build started, max time: 14400 seconds

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1: Code-Review-2
> > 
> > And here we go... BUM!
> > 
> > Less than two months since we merge:
> > 
> > https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/101311
> > 
> > I've been complaining about this solution multiple times but no one from LFN was willing to take those comments into account "because we do it this way in all other projects" and "those are static file that will never be updated". As @Sylvain wrote in the review:
> > "I also think this is a bad solution and I keep my "I todd you so" for when I'll blow. __BUT__ everybody has moved except us so I'll merge. See you in six months / one year when this solution will fail"
> > 
> > So here we are after not 6 but only 2 months with those "static files" and having ~100 reviews:
> > 
> > https://gerrit.onap.org/r/q/CIMAN-376
> > 
> > Just to change which XSS file should be used. Don't you think sth is really wrong here? and if we merge this what we do with Frankfurt? Cherry-pick and have another 100 reviews?
> > 
> > *I'm really looking forward to have some proper solution for this as it has just been proven that the one which has been force pushed doesn't work*
> 
> Hi Krzy, 
> 
> We all understood your frustration. I agree this migration has this part as a downside but the benefits are so much better compared to submodules. 

And what are the benefits compared to a simple makefile that would just wget those files from a single repository when someone wants to build the documentation or compared to building this in a docker container that would already contain those files?

> Changes to config files are not done too often and I am willing to take the workload whenever it happens, I have committed to it. These changes were done as part of a script and did not take too much of my time to make.

I know that it's easy to automate but it's not only the matter of yours time. This is a typical synchronization problem and sooner or later those files will start to diverge between projects and what then? Gigantic sync of 100 repos?

It's a bad practice to do it in this way. We always should have single source of truth, this is what they taught all of us in universities right?

> It will take me some time to +2 and merge all, correct. But is something that I can help doing meanwhile we continue to make things better by adapting things to my script.

So now we get to the conclusion that it's not only your time but also time of people who need to review them. Time and resources needed to execute gating/build of those patches etc... I'm really curious how many jenkins worker hours we wasted in total to merge all those 100 review with such a single line change...

> We can think of adding a direct push rather than gerrit changes if think changes are small enough for example (not an official solution, im just providing an example. I would appreciate if you and the community provide their examples too on how can all make things better for docs and for teams). 
> For now, I don't see why this should be -2. Can you please provide a justification on why is this not a good change right now?

It's not a good change because the design behind it is broken and violates one of software development principles (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Single_source_of_truth). The assertion when we got the previous change presented to us was that those file are static and as this patch shows they are not. That's why I strongly believe that instead of making such change and going further with this broken design it should be fixed.

If we want ONAP to follow best software development practices why don't we follow them for the infrastructure?

> 
> As agreed in the TSC, this is not needed for Frankfurt and teams do not need to cherry-pick this change. This change will be good to have in G release however hence the reason why I am merging it now so that teams will inherit it when branching for G

Ok that's a good news thanks!

Patch Set 1:

Hi Krzysztof, first of all there is no need for this tone. If we stop for a second and go back to when we met in Prague, where you were sitting front row, really supporting this migration and offered to use the OSA project as a pilot. Not sure what happened between January and now. You are saying that you have been complaining multiple times but I don’t remember anyone speaking up during meetings or so. What I have seen is more less rude comments in Gerrit. This is open source and as far as I know, it is not fair to just shit on someone’s idea without a counter proposal or at least give some ideas/thoughts. So either we meet and try to sort this out, in a friendly manner or you will accept this solution a be a bit more supportive.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Hi Krzysztof, first of all there is no need for this tone.

My tone was never supposed to be rude. If it sound like that it was not intentional the only thing I wanted to emphasize is that "I told you" but no one was willing to listen then... (see both OSA and OOM reviews when this change was introduced)

> If we stop for a second and go back to when we met in Prague, where you were sitting front row, really supporting this migration and offered to use the OSA project as a pilot. Not sure what happened between January and now. 

The answer is nothing!:) I still fully support getting out of this complicated submodule layout that causes some errors but in the same time I'm not supporting replacing one issue with another one which we actually did.

> You are saying that you have been complaining multiple times but I don’t remember anyone speaking up during meetings or so. 

U asked multiple times questions in gerrit and they've been never taken into account because "we do it this way in other projects" so well is this the open source spirit that you talk about? You can even see that I've never gave +1 nor +2 when this change was introduced to OOM and the only reason why PTL merged this was because we've been the last project with this change and everyone else just merged it.

> What I have seen is more less rude comments in Gerrit.

My comments were never supposed to be rude but technical. If they sounded rude it's only my fault and I apologize for it.

> This is open source and as far as I know, it is not fair to just shit on someone’s idea without a counter proposal or at least give some ideas/thoughts.

You are right this is open source and we should focus on producing the best possible software thus I believe that *technical criticism* is important and should be welcomed. Saying that this design does not follow one of software development best practices is a technical argument and considering that we have the same file in almost 100 repos it's rather a fact that a feeling.

I provided several ideas earlier and also in this review (makefile that wgets those file, docker container etc). I'm not saying that any of those is perfect and I'm happy to take *any* opinion on those proposals but I want to at least have a discussion on this so that we can collectively choose technically the best solution without pushing changes with argument "we do this already in 200 repos so 100 more is not a big deal". If we want our code to be of a really high quality we cannot afford for such changes.

> So either we meet and try to sort this out, in a friendly manner or you will accept this solution a be a bit more supportive.

I'm happy to participate in any kind of discussion that is finally going to be focused on technical aspects and try to figure out together how which technical solution should be used instead of just proving the solution to be good because XX other projects merged it.

Patch Set 1:

Hello,
I tend to agree with Krzysztof and "I told you so".
Can't we just breathe and try to find a better solution than patching all the repos?

Abandoned

No activity for more than 3 months
